title: --security-opt seccomp=unconfined --privileged | bash: /dev/null: Permission denied
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**
Porting a old app to docker images, tested on docker for windows and it works fine on podman I have the below issue.

sudo podman create --name=somename --network container:othername --security-opt seccomp=unconfined --privileged mysuperimage:1.0
sudo podman start somename
sudo podman exec -it somename /bin/bash 
[root@a0c3e1fd8d5a app]# su - cli
bash: /dev/null: Permission denied
bash: /dev/null: Permission denied
bash: /dev/null: Permission denied
bash: /dev/null: Permission denied

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Create image instance with --priviledged

2. Try to switch to cli user that will trigger a certain script in background and i receive the bash: /dev/null: Permission denied

**Describe the results you received:**
Permission denied on podman on centos 8

**Describe the results you expected:**
To work as it works in docker on docker for windows.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
I remember that before the podman update it was working with an image build on docker for windows. I presume that its the build on windows or the newer version of podman.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.2.3
API Version:  3.2.3
Go Version:   go1.15.13
Built:        Sat Aug  7 20:21:48 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

comments.body:
FYI, `--privileged` implies `--security-opt seccomp=unconfined`; there's no need to use both.

Can you provide a reproducer? It's very difficult to identify what's going on unless we have can replicate the error.

Found the issue, during the build i didnt noticed that i was receiving the  following errors

mknod: /opt/cli/jail/dev/null': Operation not permitted
mknod: /opt/cli/jail/dev/pts/ptmx': Operation not permitted
mknod: /opt/cli/jail/dev/random': Operation not permitted
mknod: /opt/cli/jail/dev/tty': Operation not permitted
mknod: /opt/cli/jail/dev/urandom': Operation not permitted
mknod: /opt/cli/jail/dev/zero': Operation not permitted

by building the image with CAP_MKNOD  it worked

```
podman build --cap-add=CAP_MKNOD -t 
```
Yes we default to a tighter security then Docker, we don't allow CAP_MKNOD by default.
